title: A barber in Naples who never charged me right
summary: Tell the story of a small Naples barbershop where the price was never the same twice.
topic: A barber in Naples who never charged me right
prompt: Write 8–10 sentences (150–250 words) about a barber or hairdresser abroad who treated you in an unusual way. Use past simple and past continuous. Include 'when', 'while', and 'because'.
hintStarters: The first time I walked into his shop in…
Every visit, the price was different because…
While he was cutting my hair, he often…
sampleText: When I lived in Naples for one winter, I found a small old-fashioned barber on a narrow street behind the train station. His name was Gennaro and his shop had only two chairs and a tall mirror with cracks at the corner. I went to him for the first time because my Italian friend told me he was the best barber in the neighbourhood. The shop was not modern at all, but Gennaro used his scissors slowly and carefully, as if every haircut was important. The strange thing was the price. The first time I paid ten euros. The second time, while he was cutting my hair, he told me a long story about his brother in Argentina and at the end he only asked for seven. The third time he waved away my money and gave me a small espresso instead. I never understood his system. I think he charged each customer by mood, not by minutes. When I left Naples in March, I went one last time. Gennaro shaved my neck very carefully and said, 'Today is free, because you are leaving.' I tried to give him a tip, but he closed my hand around the coins and pushed me gently to the door.

tôi không bao giờ hiểu cách tính của ông ấy
tính tiền mỗi khách theo tâm trạng
cạo gáy cho tôi rất cẩn thận

sampleAnnotations.phrase: Today is free, because you are leaving
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Hôm nay miễn phí, vì cậu sắp rời đi

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: khép tay tôi lại quanh những đồng xu

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple vs past continuous in storytelling
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng past simple cho các sự kiện chính ('I paid', 'he told', 'he closed') và past continuous cho hành động đang diễn ra trong nền ('while he was cutting my hair'). Cấu trúc 'while + past continuous, past simple' là chuẩn mực cho câu chuyện ở A2.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple for the main events ('I paid', 'he told', 'he closed') and past continuous for an ongoing background action ('while he was cutting my hair'). The pattern 'while + past continuous, past simple' is a core A2 storytelling structure.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Connector: because
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Because' nối lý do với một mệnh đề hoàn chỉnh: 'I went to him because my friend told me he was the best.' Không dùng 'because of' trước một mệnh đề đầy đủ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Because' joins a reason as a full clause: 'I went to him because my friend told me he was the best.' Do not use 'because of' before a full clause.

sampleTranslationVi: Khi tôi sống ở Naples trong một mùa đông, tôi tìm thấy một tiệm cắt tóc nhỏ kiểu cũ trên một con phố hẹp phía sau ga tàu. Ông ấy tên là Gennaro và tiệm chỉ có hai chiếc ghế và một tấm gương cao có vết nứt ở góc. Tôi đến lần đầu vì người bạn Ý của tôi nói ông ấy là thợ cắt tóc giỏi nhất trong khu phố. Tiệm không hiện đại chút nào, nhưng Gennaro dùng kéo chậm rãi và cẩn thận, như thể mỗi lần cắt đều quan trọng. Điều lạ là giá tiền. Lần đầu tôi trả mười euro. Lần thứ hai, trong khi ông ấy đang cắt tóc cho tôi, ông kể một câu chuyện dài về người anh trai ở Argentina và cuối cùng chỉ đòi bảy euro. Lần thứ ba ông khoát tay từ chối tiền của tôi và đưa cho tôi một ly espresso nhỏ thay vì lấy tiền. Tôi không bao giờ hiểu cách tính của ông ấy. Tôi nghĩ ông tính tiền mỗi khách theo tâm trạng, không theo phút. Khi tôi rời Naples vào tháng Ba, tôi đến lần cuối. Gennaro cạo gáy cho tôi rất cẩn thận và nói, 'Hôm nay miễn phí, vì cậu sắp rời đi.' Tôi cố gắng đưa ông tiền boa, nhưng ông khép tay tôi lại quanh những đồng xu và đẩy tôi nhẹ ra cửa.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple opening.
options: When I live in Naples for one winter, I find a small barber.
When I lived in Naples for one winter, I found a small barber.
When I was living in Naples for one winter, I was finding a small barber.
When I have lived in Naples for one winter, I found a small barber.
mcQuestions.explanation: Two completed past actions in a finished time take past simple: 'lived' and 'found'.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs describe completed past events.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the sentence with correct past continuous in the 'while' clause.
options: While he cut my hair, he told me a story.
While he was cutting my hair, he told me a story.
While he was cutting my hair, he was telling me a story all the time.
While he cuts my hair, he was telling me a story.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'While' + past continuous sets the background; past simple marks the event inside it.

options: to refuse politely with a hand gesture
to ask for more money
to count the money carefully
to drop the money on the floor
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Wave away' = refuse with a hand gesture.

options: I went to him because of my friend told me he was the best.
I went to him because my friend told me he was the best.
I went to him because of he was the best.
I went to him because my friend tells me he was the best.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Because' is followed by a full clause; verbs in a past story stay in past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word means a person who cuts men's hair?
options: barber
tailor
waiter
butcher
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'barber' cuts men's hair and shaves beards.
mcQuestions.hint: He cuts hair and shaves beards.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural phrase to describe a small narrow street.
options: on a wide and noisy street
on a narrow street behind the train station
on a high street with many cars
on a long beach near the sea
mcQuestions.explanation: The sample text uses 'on a narrow street behind the train station' — concrete and small-scale.
mcQuestions.hint: Match the description of a small back street.

options: broken and dirty
in a style from the past, traditional
expensive and fashionable
very modern
to give money to a customer
to ask a customer to pay a price
to clean a customer's hair
to write down an appointment
mcQuestions.explanation: To 'charge' someone means to ask them to pay a price.
mcQuestions.hint: Verb related to setting a price.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most natural reported speech.
options: He told that today is free.
He said me today was free.
He told me, 'Today is free, because you are leaving.'
He said to me that today is free, because you leave.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Tell someone' + direct quote works naturally. 'Say me' and 'told that' are wrong patterns.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence keeps tenses consistent in past?
options: I tried to give him a tip, but he closes my hand around the coins.
I try to give him a tip, but he closed my hand around the coins.
I tried to give him a tip, but he closed my hand around the coins.
I am trying to give him a tip, but he closed my hand around the coins.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs must be in past simple for two finished past actions.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the best meaning of 'regular customer'.
options: a customer who pays the full price
a customer who comes often
a customer who never tips
a customer who lives nearby
mcQuestions.explanation: A 'regular customer' is someone who returns to a business often.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever met a shopkeeper or worker who treated you differently from other customers? Why do you think they did that?
